FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class InfoBar

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI InfoBar: an inline, app-wide status surface. A severity icon (a filled circle glyph with an inverse-colored status glyph on top), a Title (SemiBold) + Message content panel (laid out horizontally when it fits on one line, vertically otherwise, like WinUI's InfoBarPanel), an optional trailing action button, and an optional close button. The background is a subtle severity tint over a card stroke. Controlled by isOpen.

public static class InfoBar

object.ToString()

Fields

PartCloseButton

The trailing 38x38 X button (WinUI CloseButton). Owned: OnClick (the Closing→onClose→Closed lifecycle chain — kept whenever the control has close handlers; a modifier OnClick survives only when it has none), Role.

public const string PartCloseButton = "CloseButton"

Field Value

string

PartIcon

The 16x16 standard severity icon Z-stack (WinUI StandardIcon area). Owned: none — the per-severity glyph colors are recomputed per render in the stock build (override-able).

public const string PartIcon = "Icon"

Field Value

string

PartMessage

The message body (WinUI Message). A TextEl part — restyle via parts.Set<TextEl>(InfoBar.PartMessage, t => t with { … }). Owned: none.

public const string PartMessage = "Message"

Field Value

string

PartRoot

The severity-tinted bar root. Owned: Children (the icon / panel / close columns — conditional mounts), Role. The severity tint/stroke are stock per-render styling a modifier may override.

public const string PartRoot = "Root"

Field Value

string

PartTitle

The SemiBold title (WinUI Title). A TextEl part — restyle via parts.Set<TextEl>(InfoBar.PartTitle, t => t with { … }). Owned: none.

public const string PartTitle = "Title"

Field Value

string

Methods

Create(InfoBarSeverity, string, string, Action?, bool, bool, bool, Element?, Action<InfoBarClosingEventArgs>?, Action<InfoBarClosedEventArgs>?, Element?, string?, TemplateParts?) 

public static Element Create(InfoBarSeverity severity, string title, string message, Action? onClose = null, bool isOpen = true, bool isClosable = true, bool isIconVisible = true, Element? actionButton = null, Action<InfoBarClosingEventArgs>? onClosing = null, Action<InfoBarClosedEventArgs>? onClosed = null, Element? content = null, string? iconGlyph = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

severity InfoBarSeverity

Selects the status glyph + severity colors via InfoBar.InfoBarTemplateSettings.

title string

SemiBold title line (WinUI InfoBarTitleFontWeight). Empty => omitted.

message string

Regular message body. Empty => omitted.

onClose Action

Invoked when the X is clicked (WinUI sets reason=CloseButton then IsOpen=false). Pair with onClosing/onClosed for the full lifecycle.

isOpen bool

Whether the bar is shown. WinUI's IsOpen defaults to false; this stateless factory defaults to true so a constructed bar renders (callers gate visibility with their own signal/state).

isClosable bool

When true (the WinUI default) the trailing 38x38 close button is shown.

isIconVisible bool

When true (the WinUI default) the standard severity icon is shown.

actionButton Element

Optional trailing action slot (WinUI ActionButton) - laid out inline after the message when it fits, else below it. Pass e.g. Button.Create(...) or HyperlinkButton.Create(...).

onClosing Action<InfoBarClosingEventArgs>

Closing hook (WinUI Closing). Set e.Cancel = true to veto: the close is aborted and onClose/onClosed do not run (the caller keeps the bar open).

onClosed Action<InfoBarClosedEventArgs>

Closed hook (WinUI Closed), fired after a non-canceled close.

content Element

Optional expanded content (WinUI Content/ContentTemplate, InfoBar.idl:94-95): rendered in the second row of the content column, below the title/message/action panel (ContentArea, InfoBar.xaml:126). When title, message AND action are all absent it becomes the sole content of the column (the NoBannerContent state, InfoBar.xaml:86-90 / InfoBar.cpp:282-286).

iconGlyph string

Optional custom icon glyph (WinUI IconSource, InfoBar.idl:77). When set (and isIconVisible) it replaces the standard severity circle+status stack — the UserIconVisible state (InfoBar.cpp:261-264) — capped at 16x16 with the standard icon margin (UserIconBox, InfoBar.xaml:111).

parts TemplateParts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts — see TemplateParts for the contract. Title/Message are TextEl parts (parts.Set<TextEl>(InfoBar.PartTitle, …)).

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
